content.text: This page tests Pattern C: constrained correction near locked structural blocks. Prose edits adjacent to locked nodes must preserve the locked structure without mutation.
The expand macro and info panel below are locked_structural nodes. Headings and paragraphs around them are editable_prose.
This content is inside an expand macro. The expand node itself is locked_structural, though this inner paragraph is classified as editable_prose.
The verifier must confirm that no locked-node mutation occurs when editing adjacent prose sections.
This paragraph is adjacent to the locked expand block above. Editing this section tests that the structural preservation gate holds at 100 percent for locked nodes.
The scope selector heading:Notes should isolate this section while leaving the expand macro and panel untouched.
This info panel is a locked_structural wrapper. Its inner paragraph is editable_prose, but the panel node itself must not be mutated by adjacent edits.
Final section for Pattern C experiments. This heading provides a third scope selector target on a page with mixed editable and locked structural adjacency.
The horizontal rule above is classified as locked_structural in the current codebase, despite being listed as editable_prose in the spec. See ideas/2026-03-route-classification-drift.md.
